course_title: Advanced Special Topics in German Studies
course_description:
The course develops advanced skills in German studies, including literary appreciation, film analysis and the critical interrogation of important historical movements and philosophical directions within Germany's cultural legacy. The German-language text corpus (narrative, literary, filmic) is sufficiently flexible to cater for differing students' interests and priorities.

Through a series of guided readings and seminar-style engagement with themes and issues, theﾠcourse aims to develop understanding of German culture, history and literature. It does so by shining a light on the time of Goethe, a period which saw the greatest flowering of thought and literary expression in the history of the German nation. The course argues that Germany today cannot be fully understood without a basic comprehension of this key period and its development. The course has three parts, each of which will be individually assessed.ﾠIn the first part, we will read some ofﾠGoethe's poetry, particularly that drawn fromﾠthe movement of "Sturm und Drang" ("Storm and Stress") to which Goethe was a major contributor.ﾠIn the second part of the course, the story Der blonde Eckbert by Ludwig Tieck, one of the most important representatives of German Romanticism, will be the focus. In the third and finalﾠpart, the course will consider Heinrich von Kleist, the greatest writer of short stories in the German tongue. A close reading of Kleist's famous story Das Erdbeben in Chili, servingﾠas a review ofﾠkey ideas that emerged during theﾠ"Goethezeit",ﾠwill be undertaken. Regular attendance, evidence ofﾠthorough preparation and a willingness to interact inﾠsmall-group discussion are essential forﾠthe successful completion of this course.

Class allocation:
In order to optimise the student experience, it may be necessary to reallocate students to a different class from their first choice.
Before this happens, every effort will be made to enable students to voluntarily change into an alternative class that is suitable.
Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation. Therefore, should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please ensure you email

from your UQ student email account with the following details:

the Course Code

assessment_summary.title: In-class Presentation
assessment_summary.due_date: Presentation 9/03/2026 - 23/03/2026; Summary 27/03/2026; Presentation:; consult with lecturer to confirm choice of poem and to schedule in-class presentation date (WEEK 3 to WEEK 5).; Summary:; due 5pm Friday, WEEK 5

assessment_details.title: In-class Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: Presentation 9/03/2026 - 23/03/2026 Summary 27/03/2026 Presentation: consult with lecturer to confirm choice of poem and to schedule in-class presentation date (WEEK 3 to WEEK 5). Summary: due 5pm Friday, WEEK 5
assessment_details.task_description: This component requires a 10 minute in-class oral presentation in German, to be written up as a 250-word written summary (also in German) after the presentation. Both the presentation and the summary are to focus on one poem by Goethe only . In the oral part of the presentation you may refer to notes, but do not merely read them out aloud. Your presentation (and the subsequent write-up of the presentation) should answer directly the four questions that appear below: 1. Wann hat Goethe dieses Gedicht geschrieben? 2. Wo/wann/in welchem Umfeld/Kontext spielt das Gedicht? 3. Was thematisiert das Gedicht? 4. Was möchte der Dichter mit dem Gedicht sagen? The 10 minute presentation is to be completed in class between Week 3 and Week 5. Students may present a maximum of 10 powerpoint slides during the presentation. The 250-word summary in German is to be submitted no later than 5pm on March 27, 2026 and is to be submitted via Turnitin. No bibliography is required, but a brief list of references may be included if you have referred to one or more secondary readings in your presentation. The grading criteria for the presentation and summary can be found in Blackboard Assesment Folder (GRMN3330 Presentation Write-Up Rubric). Sign up for your presentation in class after consulting the lecturer. Use of Generative AI & MT: The oral, in-class presentation of a poem in German is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. For the written submission, students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The 250-word summary in German is to be submitted no later than 5pm on March 27, 2026 and is to be submitted via Turnitin. No bibliography is required, but a brief list of references may be included if you have referred to one or more secondary readings in your presentation.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.title: In-Class Written Exam
assessment_details.task_description: The exam will be conducted in-class in Week 9 and will last up to 90 minutes. What is involved? The exam will have two parts. The first part of the exam will focus on questions relating to the narrative plot. These questions are to be answered in German. The second part of the exam will focus on questions of interpretation. Students may answer this part of the exam in English or German. No bibliography for this task is required. Use of Generative AI & MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: End of Semester Written Exam
assessment_details.task_description: The exam will have two parts. Part One will focus on the third text studied in class, Kleist's short story "Das Erdbeben in Chili". Part Two will invite a reflection on the themes of the course and will cover all the material studied in class. Questions for both parts of the exam may be answered in English or German. Use of Generative AI & MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Interactive tutorials DISCUSSION OF KEY CONCEPTS: Conceptual understanding developed through interactive lecture-style activities, which also include student presentations. INTERACTIVE ACTIVITIES: Students will be introduced to and engaged in the critical analysis of key concepts of the course topics Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
